confidence.formula_text: Base confidence starts at 0.5. Add 0.1 for each correlated independent signal up to a maximum addition of 0.4. Add 0.1 if an active weather alert from PMD API is confirmed. Subtract 0.1 if all signals are older than 20 minutes. Subtract 0.1 if only one source type is represented. The minimum possible score is 0.2. The maximum possible score is 1.0.

delayed rescue access if congestion is not reduced
reasoning_chain: Signal count is 3 correlated crisis signal(s) from 3 total observed signal(s) within G-10 over 1.0 minutes.
Weather evidence: active rainfall alert level 2 confirmed from simulated PMD API; rainfall is 5.2mm per hour.
Traffic evidence: Srinagar Highway G-10 underpass speed 7.0km/h versus normal 50.0km/h, congestion 4/5.
Residential population density for G-10 is estimated at 8400 per square kilometre.
Severity logic: severity score is 4 of 5 because crisis_type=urban_flooding, escalation=crisis, weather_alert=True, affected_roads=1, and max_urgency=4.
Confidence formula: 0.5 base + 0.3 signal addition (min(0.1 * 3, 0.4)) + 0.1 weather-alert addition - 0.0 stale-signal penalty - 0.0 single-source penalty = 0.9, bounded to final score 0.9.
Primary risks: vehicle entrapment; infrastructure damage from standing floodwater; traffic immobilisation on Srinagar Highway G-10 underpass
Secondary risks: displacement pressure for low-income residents near G-10; delayed rescue access if congestion is not reduced
Escalation decision remains crisis; system state is crisis active.
